mission.title: Install and Configure Redis Operator (Spotahome / OT-CONTAINER-KIT)
mission.description: The Redis Operator by OT-CONTAINER-KIT simplifies the deployment and management of Redis clusters on Kubernetes. It supports various Redis configurations, including standalone, cluster, and sentinel modes, while providing built-in monitoring capabilities. This operator is ideal for users looking for a production-ready solution to manage Redis workloads with best practices.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Redis Operator Helm repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add redis-operator https://ot-container-kit.github.io/redis-operator

```

> This command adds the repository where the Redis Operator charts are hosted.

steps.title: Install the Redis Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Redis Operator in the specified namespace using Helm.

```bash

helm install redis-operator redis-operator/redis-operator --namespace redis --create-namespace --version 0.24.0

```

> This command installs the Redis Operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Create a Redis Cluster Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Custom Resource (CR) to define the desired state of your Redis cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: redis.opstreelabs.in/v1
kind: Redis
metadata:
  name: my-redis
  namespace: redis
spec:
  size: 3
  redis:
    image: redis:7.0.0
EOF

```

> This command creates a Redis cluster with 3 replicas.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: redis.opstreelabs.in/v1
kind: Redis
metadata:
  name: my-redis
  namespace: redis
spec:
  size: 3
  redis:
    image: redis:7.0.0
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Redis Cluster
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Redis cluster to ensure it is running as expected.

```bash

kubectl get redis -n redis

```

> This command lists the Redis resources in the specified namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Redis Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Redis Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all Redis clusters managed by the operator, leading to potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall redis-operator --namespace redis

kubectl delete namespace redis

```

> This command uninstalls the Redis Operator from the specified namespace.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Redis Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your Redis data. This step will upgrade the Redis Operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get redis -n redis -o json > redis-backup.json

helm upgrade redis-operator redis-operator/redis-operator --namespace redis --version 0.24.0

kubectl get redis -n redis

```

> This command backs up the current Redis configuration.
> This command upgrades the Redis Operator to the specified version.
> This command verifies that the Redis resources are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get redis -n redis -o json > redis-backup.json

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create Redis cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient resources or misconfiguration in the Custom Resource.

**Fix:**
Check the resource limits and ensure the cluster has enough CPU and memory allocated.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Redis pod is in CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates an issue with the Redis configuration or image.

**Fix:**
Inspect the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n redis' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to Redis'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the Redis service is not properly exposed or the network policies are blocking access.

**Fix:**
Ensure the Redis service is running and check the network policies in place.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Redis operator not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Redis Operator is not installed or is not running.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the Redis Operator using the Helm install command.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Redis 7.0, improved monitoring capabilities with redis-exporter.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps are required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Redis cluster listed with the command 'kubectl get redis -n redis', showing the status as 'Running'.

apiVersion: redis.opstreelabs.in/v1
kind: Redis
metadata:
  name: my-redis
  namespace: redis
spec:
  size: 3
  redis:
    image: redis:7.0.0
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
